page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the c3 server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores with hyper-threading running at 2.7 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD for storage, but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The underlying Intel Xeon CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1617.89 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1714.0. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3323.04 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3812.0, reflecting the physical core limit of the 4-vCPU configuration.

Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with both read and write large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks falling into the bottom 25 percent, yielding 29.2 GB/sec read and 22.26 GB/sec write. Despite these memory constraints, the server achieves average throughput in static web serving at 6.5 GB/sec and single-threaded gzip compression at 26.36 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are weak, indicating that memory-bound or highly concurrent database workloads may face performance limitations.

Given the hardware profile, this server offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that require fast local scratch space, thanks to the bundled 402 GB NVMe SSD. It is well-suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and small-scale text processing. However, it is less efficient for heavy multi-threaded compilation, complex ray tracing, or memory-intensive database operations due to its lower multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark results.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is a general-purpose server configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD. It features advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmark data indicates average performance in single-core CPU tasks, single-threaded compression, and static web serving, which reaches 6.5 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with large-block read bandwidth at 29.2 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 22.26 GB/sec. For example, its Geekbench single-core score is 1714.0, whereas its multi-core score of 3812.0 falls into the lower tier. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight web serving, and applications requiring fast local scratch storage, though it presents performance tradeoffs for memory-intensive or highly concurrent database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD, offering balanced general-purpose performance with fast local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4-lssd is a 4-vCPU, 16 GB RAM server with a 402 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, balanced memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory paired with a 402 GB local NVMe SSD
Supports advanced hardware instructions including AVX-512 and AMX
Average single-core CPU and static web serving performance
Lower multi-core and memory bandwidth efficiency under heavy workloads
Suitable for lightweight web serving, text processing, and local scratch space tasks
